name: John Wesley Ame Zion Church
description_short: John Wesley AME Zion Church Federal Credit Union is a Washington, DC-based credit union serving over 100 members since 1976 with $76,000 in assets.
description_long:
John Wesley AME Zion Church Federal Credit Union was established in 1976 and has operated for nearly 50 years as a faith-based financial institution serving its members in the Washington, DC area. The credit union is affiliated with the John Wesley AME Zion Church and operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution.

The credit union offers standard banking services to its membership base of over 100 members. Members can join the institution and access banking services through their two physical branch locations—one at the head office in Northwest Washington, DC, and another in Upper Marlboro, Maryland. The institution maintains assets totaling $76,000 and accepts new member applications through their "Join now" option on their website.

As a federally chartered credit union, this institution distinguishes itself by serving a specific community tied to the John Wesley AME Zion Church. The organization emphasizes membership-based banking rather than profit-maximization, reflecting the cooperative credit union model. Their relatively small asset base and membership numbers indicate a hyper-local, community-focused operation rather than a regional or national financial institution.

This credit union is best suited for members seeking to maintain banking relationships within their faith community. However, prospective members should be aware that the institution's small size (76K in assets and 100+ members) means limited product offerings and infrastructure compared to larger credit unions or banks. The specific membership requirements and eligibility criteria are not detailed on the website, which may restrict access to the general public. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: In-person banking at head office (1615 14th St NW, Washington, DC)
In-person banking at branch location (Upper Marlboro, MD)
pros: Established financial institution with 48+ years of operational history since 1976
Faith-based community credit union serving members of John Wesley AME Zion Church
Two physical branch locations in DC and Maryland for member access
Member-owned cooperative structure emphasizing community over profit
Accepts new membership applications for qualified individuals
Maintains federal credit union insurance protections for member deposits
cons: Very small asset base of only $76,000 limits product offerings and financial stability
Membership limited to 100+ people suggests restricted eligibility criteria not publicly detailed
No information provided about specific services, loan products, or account types available
Limited online presence and no mention of digital banking, mobile apps, or online services
Small size may mean limited customer service hours and fewer amenities than larger institutions
best_for: Members of John Wesley AME Zion Church seeking to bank within their faith community
Individuals wanting to support a local, community-based financial institution
People with existing membership eligibility seeking basic banking services
diagnosis: This credit union is best for individuals who are members of or affiliated with John Wesley AME Zion Church and seek basic banking services within a faith-based community context. The primary caveat is that the institution's extremely small asset base ($76K) and undisclosed membership requirements mean it likely operates with limited product variety and may not serve the general public without church affiliation.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
